entry: Added intel-cleverhans-worm (arXiv 2606.03811): AI-driven adaptive worm with recursive per-target reasoning on open-weight LLM. Corroborating evidence across ao-1, ao-2, ao-5, cl-3, ev-1 — no gap status changes. Added Docs tab to tracker.
Moved pc-2 (selective data ID by LLM) from PARTIAL to EXISTS on in-the-wild evidence: PromptSteal (APT28) and QuietVault. Added intel-promptsteal.
Moved ao-3 (environment value assessment) from GAP to PARTIAL on POC-grade evidence: Ransomware 3.0 (NYU Tandon). Added intel-ransomware3. Labelled POC-grade.
Recorded deliberate HOLD of ip-1 at GAP despite accumulating supporting evidence; mission-objective IP targeting under adversarial conditions remains undemonstrated.

Added intel-miasma (commoditised Mini Shai-Hulud variant), intel-clawchain (OpenClaw agent takeover), intel-agent-hijack (steal-the-orchestration-layer pattern), and intel-aisi-pan-benchmark (controlled multi-stage intrusion). No gap moves; supporting weight for c2-1/c2-4, cl-3/cl-4/pc-1, and thesis-level refinement of the orchestration-layer assumption.

Post-Infosec Europe review: 8 additional intelligence citations added — APT34/35/MuddyWater C2, GTIG GTG-1002 autonomous ops, frontier model trajectory, 80% ransomware AI adoption, 76% polymorphic malware, Lumu AI-C2 prediction. Total: 25 documents.
Presented framework at Infosec Europe. Published architecture and gap analysis. Added Horizon3 (NodeZero), PentAGI, and Node4 citations.
Mini Shai-Hulud Wave 5 case study added. Five-wave evolution mapped to Hydra architecture.
GTIG AI Threat Tracker published. Three gaps moved from RED to AMBER: zero-day weaponisation, dynamic C2 rotation, AI real-time evasion.
Gap analysis completed. 34 capabilities assessed: 17 exist, 9 partial, 8 gaps.
Anthropic Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing findings integrated into framework.
AI Hydra framework created. Operational architecture defined: operator control plane, AI orchestration engine, hybrid C2 backbone, tool/TTP layer, and adversary SDLC weapons foundry.

Think tank working group established to review the threat and viability of AI-enabled offensive cyber. Scope defined beyond phishing, deepfake, and malware engine evasion — specifically examining advanced autonomous capabilities including AI-driven attack orchestration, exploit development, and adaptive C2.
